sources_exist.note: All four canonical HTTP(S) citations resolve to accessible pages on the cited sites.

figures_match_sources.note: None of the four cited pages supports its full score-driving claim: the M&A index does not establish zero drafting-bureau consolidators, the AutoCAD vendor page does not report 8% or 78% adoption, the BIM history does not provide the claimed 50% timeline, and the offshore staffing page publishes no hourly rates or US billing mix.

judgment_inputs_plausible.note: The prompt permits rebuilding the 4-digit role mix, and the declared role shares sum to one, but the 85% CAD/BIM automation fraction lacks auditable task evidence. The 20% adoption baseline, 4.5-year t50, 45% older-owner share, 2.5x buy multiple, and 4.0x exit multiple are unsupported estimates, while pi_dist and pi_moat rely on unverified rate and insourcing assertions.

rubric_consistent.note: The run uses the supplied deterministic dataset inputs and addresses the drafting-specific terminal question, but V, A, B, C, and M all depend on unsupported score-driving assumptions. The LOW_CONFIDENCE flag is confirmed and remains unresolved. The ESTIMATE_HEAVY flag is confirmed for ai_replaceable_share, buy_mult, current_adoption_pct, exit_mult, owners_60plus_pct, and t50_years. The ARITH_DELTA_WITHIN_TOL flag is acknowledged for A, B, and S; small arithmetic deltas do not cure the evidentiary failures.

cross_checks_honest.note: The no-proxy section discloses the hypothesis role rebuild, but the pricing check presents $10-30 offshore rates, pure piece-rate billing, immediate pass-through, and client insourcing as established facts even though the cited page provides no prices or billing evidence. The terminal check likewise assigns a 5-15% surviving scan-to-BIM niche and a 2-5-year collapse window without canonical support.

source_audits.note: The page is a broad index of architecture and engineering M&A updates. It contains no 541340 or drafting-bureau analysis and cannot establish that a systematic search found zero PE-backed platforms actively acquiring US drafting service bureaus in the specified 24-month window.

The page markets Energent's AutoCAD-related AI solution and describes product benchmarks and a block-replacement case study. It does not contain the quoted 8% firm-integration or 78% interest figures, identify a survey population, or support 20% material AI adoption among US 541340 firms.

The article gives a qualitative BIM history, including early technical origins and later government mandates. It does not report near-zero adoption in 2002-2004, approximately 50% adoption by 2009-2010, a seven-to-eight-year time to 50%, or faster CAD adoption by drafting firms.

The OffshorePH staffing page says costs vary with project complexity, software expertise, and experience level and directs users to request a quote. It publishes no India $10-25 or Philippines $12-30 hourly rates and provides no evidence for US 541340 hourly or per-sheet billing, lack of relationship lock-in, or immediate pass-through of AI savings.

reasons: At inputs.current_adoption_pct.url (https://www.energent.ai/use-cases/en/compare/ai-solution-for-autocad), the cited vendor page does not contain the quoted 8% or 78% figures and cannot support the conversion to 20% material adoption among US drafting service firms. Replace it with an accessible survey that defines the firm population and material production use.

At inputs.historical_analogs.url (https://vdci.edu/blog/the-evolution-and-adoption-of-bim-software-around-the-world), the qualitative history does not provide the claimed near-zero-to-50% BIM adoption observations or drafting-firm CAD curve. Add dated adoption measurements before deriving inputs.t50_years.

At inputs.pricing_structure.url (https://www.offshoreph.com/our-services/engineering-and-construction-support/draftsmen/), the page gives no price card and says rates vary. Supply accessible rate evidence and US 541340 billing-mix evidence before assigning pi_dist=0.10 or claiming pure hourly/piece-rate deflation.

At inputs.active_consolidators.url (https://www.morrisseygoodale.com/resources/ma-update/), a general A/E deal index cannot prove the absence of 541340 acquirers. Document the complete search method and exact-window target classification, including evidence for each candidate platform, before setting active_consolidators=0.

At inputs.buy_mult, the URL is empty and the source text supplies no auditable transaction set for the claimed 2.5x normalized EBITDA multiple. Add recent drafting-bureau transactions or a transparent, source-supported normalization from genuinely comparable micro-cap professional-services deals.

At inputs.exit_mult, the URL is empty and the run acknowledges that no exit-size drafting platform was identified. A theoretical 4.0x strategic multiple is not a sourced exit comp; provide matching-scope transactions or treat the multiple gap and M score as unsubstantiated.

At inputs.owners_60plus_pct, the URL is empty and the 45% estimate is inferred from an uncited AEC CEO-age analogy and firm-size narrative. Add owner-level 541340 demographic evidence, or a clearly sourced and calibrated proxy, before using it in B.

At inputs.ai_replaceable_share.breakdown_by_role, the hypothesis rebuild assigns 60% of wages to CAD/BIM technicians and 85% automation within that role without canonical task-level capability evidence. Add current production-workflow benchmarks and support for the 541340 role shares before accepting V=10.

At cross_checks.terminal_value and inputs.pi_moat, the claimed 2-5-year demand collapse, 5-15% surviving field-capture niche, lack of relationship barriers, and client ability to bypass bureaus are score- and terminal-driving but have no canonical support. Add current Autodesk capability evidence, buyer-workflow evidence, and revenue-mix data, then recalibrate the terminal class and moat.
flags_reviewed: ESTIMATE_HEAVY: 6 inputs with quality ESTIMATE (ai_replaceable_share, buy_mult, current_adoption_pct, exit_mult, owners_60plus_pct, t50_years)
ARITH_DELTA_WITHIN_TOL: nonzero stored-vs-recomputed deltas on A, B, S
